text: This is a video showing a robotic S-III segmentectomy on the right side. Here represents a case of a patient with metastatic nodule in the anterior segment of the right upper lobe. The primary tumor was a colon cancer with a liver metastasis. Both tumors had been resected in two thousand and fifteen and a pulmonary nodule was detected one year later. As the nodule presented an enlargement, we decided to remove it.

The CT scan displays a centrally located pulmonary nodule. The surgeon begins by dissecting the inferior pulmonary ligament and performing a mediastinal lymphadenectomy. Levels nine and eight lymph nodes are resected, followed by levels eight and seven lymph nodes from a posterior view of the hilum.
The surgeon begins resecting lymph nodes from level ten, followed by levels two and four, using robotic instruments in the right upper lobe.

The surgeon uses an anterior approach to dissect the superior pulmonary vein, identifying its tributaries from the right upper lobe. The dissection of the fissure reveals the pulmonary artery, with visible branches leading to the middle lobe on the right side. A bladder catheter is used to guide the stapler in dividing the horizontal fissure towards the anterior hilum.
The surgeon faces the anterior part of the hilum, individually dissecting the veins of the anterior segment. The veins are then divided using a surgical stapler.

The surgeon carefully removes lymph nodes during dissection to isolate the arterial branch and segmentary bronchus. The arterial branch is visible in the foreground, with the segmentary bronchus behind it. The segmentary artery is divided using a stapler with a curved tip load, followed by dissection of the segmentary bronchus.
The surgeon uses a vessel loop to retract the segmentary bronchus, facilitating the introduction of the stapler. The segmentary bronchus is then divided using a vascular load with curved teeth.
The surgeon checks the position of the anterior segment before proceeding with stapling. After stapling, the lung expansion is carefully assessed to ensure proper function.
